title: Network Mapper
learning_objectives: Calculate subnets and identify network boundaries
Map network zones and their security functions
Identify lateral movement paths from an attacker's perspective
key_topics: Network zone identification (DMZ, internal, guest, management)
scenario: You are given a corporate network diagram for a medium-sized company. Your job is to identify network zones, calculate subnets, and determine the most likely lateral movement paths from an initial compromise in the guest WiFi zone.

scenario_context.challenge: Subnet analysis and lateral movement path identification
scenario_context.relevance: Understanding network architecture is critical for both attackers planning movement and defenders building segmentation. Many African corporate networks have flat architectures with poor segmentation.

subtasks.title: Subnet Calculation
subtasks.description: For each zone in the provided diagram, calculate the subnet, usable host range, and broadcast address.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Convert CIDR to subnet mask. Calculate network address, broadcast address, and usable range for each zone. Verify your math with ipcalc if available.
subtasks.evidence_required: Subnet calculation table for all zones

subtasks.title: Zone Mapping
subtasks.description: Identify each zone's purpose (DMZ, internal, guest, management) and list expected services.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: For each zone: what is its function? What services should be running there? What kind of traffic should flow in and out? Identify any zones that seem misconfigured.
subtasks.evidence_required: Zone purpose map with expected services

subtasks.title: Attack Path Analysis
subtasks.description: From the guest WiFi zone, map 2-3 lateral movement paths to the server zone. Note what controls would block each path.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Think like an attacker: from guest WiFi, how do you reach internal servers? Consider: ARP spoofing, VLAN hopping, credential theft, pivoting through compromised hosts. For each path, note which controls (firewalls, ACLs, NAC) would block it.
subtasks.evidence_required: Lateral movement path diagram with control gaps

reflection_prompt: The company has a completely flat network — no VLANs, no segmentation. An attacker compromises one workstation. Describe the impact and design a segmentation plan they could implement within 30 days.

quiz.question: A /24 subnet has how many usable host addresses?
quiz.explanation: A /24 has 256 total addresses, minus network address and broadcast address = 254 usable hosts.

quiz.question: Lateral movement from guest WiFi to internal servers is easier when:
options: Network segmentation is weak or absent
quiz.explanation: Without proper segmentation, an attacker in the guest WiFi zone may be able to reach internal resources directly.

mentor_notes: Subnet math must be correct — no partial credit. The attack path analysis reveals whether the student thinks like an attacker. Look for creative paths beyond the obvious.
